# kong-gateway_rbac_user (Resource)

RbacUser Resource

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "kong-gateway_rbac_user" "my_rbacuser" {
  comment          = "...my_comment..."
  created_at       = 2
  enabled          = false
  id               = "...my_id..."
  name             = "...my_name..."
  updated_at       = 1
  user_token       = "...my_user_token..."
  user_token_ident = "...my_user_token_ident..."
  workspace        = "team-payments"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `comment` (String) Any comments associated with the user. Requires replacement if changed.
- `created_at` (Number) Unix epoch when the resource was created. Requires replacement if changed.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Wether or not the user has RBAC enabled. Requires replacement if changed.
- `id` (String) A string representing a UUID (universally unique identifier). Requires replacement if changed.
- `name` (String) The name of the user. Requires replacement if changed.
- `updated_at` (Number) Unix epoch when the resource was last updated. Requires replacement if changed.
- `user_token` (String) Requires replacement if changed.
- `user_token_ident` (String) The user token. Requires replacement if changed.
- `workspace` (String) The name of the workspace. Default: "default"; Requires replacement if changed.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = kong-gateway_rbac_user.my_kong-gateway_rbac_user
  id = jsonencode({
    id        = ""
    workspace = "team-payments"
  })
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import kong-gateway_rbac_user.my_kong-gateway_rbac_user '{"id": "", "workspace": "team-payments"}'
```
